title: Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical remote-sensing literature for this principle.

E.description: Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI): radio interferometry produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.radio_antenna_array -> L.cross_correlation -> L.fourier_synthesis -> int.spatial, with spatially-projected accumulation and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D brightness temperature. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~30; calibration-level mismatch (ionospheric_delay, atomic_clock_drift, station_position_error) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: V(u,v) = integral I(l,m) * exp(-2*pi*i*(u*l + v*m)) dl dm; sparse (u,v) sampling
E.world_state_x: Scene-side quantity of interest (reflectance / velocity / phase / concentration)
E.observation_y: Sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D brightness temperature is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 30); ionospheric_delay dominates the stability cliff; atomic_clock_drift and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while TV / wavelet-sparsity / deep priors stabilise recovery at the ill-conditioned end of Omega.

C.solver_class: iterative projection (ADMM / GAP) + optimisation [CLEAN, MS-CLEAN] | linear-operator + convex optimisation [Compressed-Sensing-VLBI, EHT-RML]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |ionospheric_delay| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
